articles.headline: Kane eyes Lewandowski record: Tracking Europe's major goal, assist records
articles.description: Harry Kane is chasing Robert Lewandowski's incredible Bundesliga goal tally, while Kylian Mbappé seeks Champions League history. Here's the latest in Europe's record goal and assist tallies.

images.name: Harry Kane [1296x729]
images.caption: Kane scored his 20th Bundesliga goal of the season today after just 16 games.
images.credit: Photo by Tom Weller/picture alliance via Getty Images

images.name: Manchester United's Bruno Fernandes [1296x729]
images.caption: Bruno Fernandes' assist tally took a sizeable leap after Ruben Amorim was sacked as Manchester United manager.

images.name: Lamine Yamal, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Neither Kylian Mbappé nor Lamine Yamal are likely to break Lionel Messi's single-season goal and assist marks in LaLiga.

images.name: Inter Milan's Lautaro Martinez [1296x729]
images.caption: Lautaro Martinez easily leads the Seire A scoring chart, but he won't get near the single-season record.

images.name: Mason Greenwood aug 2025 [1296x729]
images.caption: Mason Greenwood was made to share the Ligue 1 top scorer title last season.

images.name: Vinicus Junior, Kylian Mbappe [1296x729]
images.caption: Kylian Mbappé is within reach of Cristiano Ronaldo's single-season Champions League goal record.
images.credit: David S. Bustamante/Real Madrid via Getty Images

images.name: Bayern Munich holds on for 3-2 win
images.caption: Harry Kane scores a brace as Bayern Munich beat Eintracht Frankfurt 3-2.

articles.headline: Transfer rumors, news: Man United keeping tabs on Stuttgart's Andrés
articles.description: Manchester United could look to the Bundesliga for their newest midfield signing. Transfer Talk has the latest.

images.name: Chema Andrés [1296x729]
images.credit: (Photo by Harry Langer/picture alliance via Getty Images)

images.name: Is Lamine Yamal the best player in the world?
images.caption: The 'ESPN FC' crew react to Lamine Yamal becoming the youngest player to score a LaLiga hat-trick this century as Barcelona beat Villarreal 4-1.

articles.headline: Arsenal win ugly vs. Chelsea; Bayern close in on Bundesliga title; more
articles.description: Arsenal muscled past Chelsea on Sunday, but coach Mikel Arteta can't be happy at how difficult things look for his team. Also: Bayern Munich likely wrapped up the Bundesliga with a Der Klassiker win.

images.name: Arsenal celeb v Chelsea 260302 [1296x729]
images.credit: Stuart MacFarlane/Arsenal FC via Getty Images

images.name: Can Arsenal ride set-pieces all the way to the title?
images.caption: Shaka Hislop and Steve Nicol wonder if Arsenal are too reliant on set-pieces to win the Premier League title.

keyEvents.text: First Half begins.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 0, FC Augsburg 1. Rubén Vargas (FC Augsburg) right footed shot from the right side of the box to the top right corner. Assisted by Ermedin Demirovic with a through ball.
Second Half begins Eintracht Frankfurt 0, FC Augsburg 1.
Arne Engels (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 1, FC Augsburg 1. Farès Chaïbi (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Junior Dina Ebimbe.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Pep Biel replaces Rubén Vargas.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 2, FC Augsburg 1. Hugo Ekitike (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner.
Ermedin Demirovic (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Patric Pfeiffer replaces Phillip Tietz.
Jeffrey Gouweleeuw (FC Augsburg) is shown the yellow card.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Niels Nkounkou replaces Philipp Max.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Ansgar Knauff replaces Junior Dina Ebimbe.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Hugo Larsson replaces Hugo Ekitike.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Tim Breithaupt replaces Kristijan Jakic.
Substitution, FC Augsburg. Sven Michel replaces Arne Engels.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Hrvoje Smolcic replaces Aurélio Buta.
Substitution, Eintracht Frankfurt. Nacho Ferri replaces Mario Götze.
Robin Koch (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Ansgar Knauff (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card.
Goal!  Eintracht Frankfurt 3, FC Augsburg 1. Omar Marmoush (Eintracht Frankfurt) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal    following a fast break.
Omar Marmoush (Eintracht Frankfurt) is shown the yellow card for excessive celebration.
Second Half ends, Eintracht Frankfurt 3, FC Augsburg 1.

article.story:
Eintracht Frankfurt took a big stride towards European football next season as they came from a goal down to secure a 3-1 Bundesliga victory over FC Augsburg on Friday.

The result strengthened Frankfurt's bid to secure a spot in the Europa Conference League qualifiers next season as they are sixth in the standings with 45 points. They are six points ahead seventh-placed Augsburg.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, NWSL more (U.S.)

Ruben Vargas opened the scoring for Augsburg in the 13th minute as he latched on to a pass inside the box and slotted it into the net from an acute angle.

Following the interval, Frankfurt played with more purpose and Junior Dina Ebimbe delivered a well-timed cross to an unmarked Fares Chaibi, who slotted in the equaliser from close range.

The hosts completed the turnaround after 61 minutes when Hugo Ekitike, on loan from Paris St Germain, unleashed a low shot into the net from the edge of the box.

As Augsburg sent every player up front for a last-ditch attempt at finding an equaliser late into stoppage time, Frankfurt managed to get the ball to Omar Marmoush and the Egyptian striker fired into an open net to complete the 3-1 win.
